content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 23, 1992


Edward Duddy
City Attorney
City of Moreno Valley
3750 University Avenue, Suite 550
Riverside, CA 92501

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-92-409

Dear Mr. Duddy:

You have requested advice on behalf of Moreno Valley City
Councilmember Bonnie Flickinger regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Your letter
does not seek advice regarding a specific pending governmental
decision and your question is general in nature. Accordingly, we
treat your letter as a request for informal assistance pursuant to
the provisions of Regulation 18239.² Additionally, our advice is
prospective in nature and we do not comment on any past conduct.

Ms. Flickinger is employed by a law firm which often provides
representation to persons coming before the city council. You
would like to know if this presents a conflict of interest for
Ms. Flickinger. We concur with the analysis in your June 29,
1992, memorandum to Councilmember Flickinger, which you also
provided to us. If the law firm has an indirect interest in a
decision, materiality must be assessed under Regulation 18702.2.
If the law firm has a direct interest in a decision, Regulation
18702.1 requires that Ms. Flickinger disqualify herself from
participating in the decision.

___________________________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Section 18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-92-409
Page 2


As you stated in your memorandum, materiality must be
determined on a case-by-case basis each time it is reasonably
foreseeable that a decision before the city council will have an
indirect effect on the law firm.

I trust this answers your question.³

Sincerely,

Jill Stecher
Counsel, Legal Division

JS:aa




3    Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.

sections.question: Ms. Flickinger is employed by a law firm which often provides representation to persons coming before the city council. You would like to know if this presents a conflict of interest for Ms. Flickinger.
sections.conclusion: We concur with the analysis in your June 29, 1992, memorandum to Councilmember Flickinger, which you also provided to us. If the law firm has an indirect interest in a decision, materiality must be assessed under Regulation 18702.2. If the law firm has a direct interest in a decision, Regulation 18702.1 requires that Ms. Flickinger disqualify herself from participating in the decision. As you stated in your memorandum, materiality must be determined on a case-by-case basis each time it is reasonably foreseeable that a decision before the city council will have an indirect effect on the law firm.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a city councilmember's employment at a law firm that regularly represents clients before the city council create a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A conflict of interest exists requiring disqualification if the law firm has a direct interest in a city council decision; if the law firm has only an indirect interest, materiality must be assessed case-by-case under Regulation 18702.2, with the councilmember disqualifying herself when appropriate.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal advice letter (not formal written advice); prospective analysis only, no past conduct reviewed; concurs with prior city attorney memorandum; clear Q and C sections present though not explicitly labeled as such.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Ms. Flickinger is employed by a law firm which often provides representation to persons coming before the city council. You would like to know if this presents a conflict of interest for Ms. Flickinger.

CONCLUSION: We concur with the analysis in your June 29, 1992, memorandum to Councilmember Flickinger, which you also provided to us. If the law firm has an indirect interest in a decision, materiality must be assessed under Regulation 18702.2. If the law firm has a direct interest in a decision, Regulation 18702.1 requires that Ms. Flickinger disqualify herself from participating in the decision. As you stated in your memorandum, materiality must be determined on a case-by-case basis each time it is reasonably foreseeable that a decision before the city council will have an indirect effect on the law firm.

embedding.summary: FPPC provides informal advice to the City Attorney of Moreno Valley regarding whether Councilmember Flickinger's employment at a law firm that represents clients before the city council creates a conflict of interest. The Commission concurs that disqualification is required for direct interests and materiality assessment is required on a case-by-case basis for indirect interests.
